title: Chapter 14 Quiz: Database with PDO
description: Test your knowledge of PDO and database interactions!

questions.question: What does PDO stand for?
questions.explanation: PDO stands for PHP Data Objects.

questions.question: PDO only works with MySQL databases.
questions.explanation: PDO supports multiple databases (MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite, etc.).

questions.question: What is the correct DSN for MySQL connection?
questions.explanation: The DSN format is 'mysql:host=HOST;dbname=NAME'.

questions.question: Which PDO attribute should you set to make it throw exceptions on errors?
questions.explanation: Set PDO::ATTR_ERRMODE to PDO::ERRMODE_EXCEPTION.

questions.question: query() should be used for queries with user input.
questions.explanation: Use prepared statements for user input to prevent SQL injection.

questions.question: What does prepare() return?
questions.explanation: prepare() returns a PDOStatement object that you can then execute.

questions.question: Which method executes a prepared statement?
questions.explanation: execute() runs a prepared statement with the given values.

questions.question: Positional placeholders are marked with a colon (e.g., :name).
questions.explanation: Named placeholders use colon; positional use '?'.

questions.question: How do you get the ID of the last inserted row?
questions.explanation: PDO::lastInsertId() returns the last auto-generated ID.

questions.question: Which method returns the number of rows affected by an INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE?
questions.explanation: rowCount() returns the number of affected rows.

questions.question: PDO::FETCH_ASSOC returns rows as objects.
questions.explanation: FETCH_ASSOC returns associative arrays.

questions.question: Which fetch mode returns an anonymous object?
questions.explanation: FETCH_OBJ returns each row as an object.

questions.question: What does beginTransaction() do?
options: Starts a new transaction, turning off autocommit
questions.explanation: beginTransaction() starts a transaction; autocommit is disabled until commit/rollback.

questions.question: commit() saves all changes made during the transaction.
questions.explanation: Yes, commit makes the changes permanent.

questions.question: What happens if you call rollBack()?
options: All changes since beginTransaction are undone
questions.explanation: rollBack() reverts all changes made during the transaction.

questions.question: Which exception class is thrown by PDO?
questions.explanation: PDO throws PDOException on errors (if exception mode is enabled).

questions.question: You should always disable PDO::ATTR_EMULATE_PREPARES for security.
questions.explanation: Disabling emulation ensures true prepared statements, which are more secure.

questions.question: Which method fetches all rows at once?
questions.explanation: fetchAll() returns an array containing all result rows.

questions.question: What does $stmt->fetch() return when no more rows are available?
questions.explanation: fetch() returns false when there are no more rows.

questions.question: PDO supports binding parameters by reference using bindParam().
questions.explanation: Yes, bindParam() binds a variable by reference; bindValue() binds by value.
